titleEn: The drone revolution in precision farming
titleZh: 精准农业中的无人机革命
summaryZh: 2026年7月21日，意大利Zeme的Cascina Scarampa将举办“Demo-Farm-Next-Vision”演示活动，聚焦空中施肥的巨大潜力。意大利先驱Aermatica3D将展示无人机在水果、蔬菜、葡萄园、果园及工业番茄等密集型作物中的精准应用。通过多光谱测绘实现变量施肥，提升效率与可持续性；同时开发了用于生物防治（如释放益虫）和温室遮阳涂层的专用套件。公司还自研了意大利制造的飞行终止系统（FTS），并预计未来数月内植保处理法规将全面放开。
translationZh:
2026年7月21日，位于Zeme（PV）的Cascina Scarampa将举办“Demo-Farm-Next-Vision”演示活动，重点展示空中施肥的巨大潜力。主要参与者包括Aermatica3D，这家意大利行业先驱将展示无人机在农业中的前沿应用。虽然农业无人机历来与大型谷物地块或稻田相关联，但Aermatica3D的解决方案如今也为果蔬行业提供了非凡且有针对性的机会，涵盖葡萄栽培、果园以及工业番茄等专业化和密集型作物。

**互补性与效率：开阔田野与行间作业**
Aermatica3D是大疆企业系统集成商和大疆农业维护中心，提供能够彻底改变作物处理的无人机机队，例如大疆Agras T100和大疆Agras T70P。这些机器能够施用生物刺激素和液体肥料，而不会造成土壤压实或踩踏。正如Aermatica3D总经理Paolo Marras（如图）在谈到这些技术在密集型农业中的应用时所说：“无人机是补充传统方法的工具，在传统设备无法作业的所有情况下变得不可或缺。”

得益于技术发展和法规变化，无人机的使用不仅在复杂的山坡葡萄园（其中像大疆Agras T25P这样的敏捷型号可以应对高达50°的坡度）中增长，而且在开阔田野中也在增长。Marras继续说道，成功的关键在于“能够以每公顷低施用量作业，这得益于分配系统的效率，该系统利用螺旋桨产生的气流来维持无人机的飞行。”

**多光谱测绘：可衡量的投资回报率**
运营效率与可持续性齐头并进。多光谱测绘可以实时评估植物活力和水分胁迫，并将数据转化为变量施用的处方图。这种向农业4.0的过渡带来了切实的投资回报率。“目标是向需要的地方施用适量的产品，这种方法节省了时间和产品，优化了处理过程，”Marras强调。满意度如此之高，以至于农场正在迅速扩大无人机的使用规模，而承包商则从一季到下一季扩大机队，以覆盖越来越大的面积。

**生物防治：无人机作为赋能技术**
除了液体之外，Aermatica3D还开发了专有套件，直接支持生物防治，例如用于昆虫卵的D-BOX、用于辅助授粉的P/G-KIT以及用于释放活体益虫的K-KIT。在这方面，Aermatica3D定期与领先的生物技术公司合作。“一个重要的例子是我们与Koppert合作，开发了一种针对无人机优化的套件，用于分发成年益虫，”Marras说，并补充道：“无人机是广泛采用生物防治技术的赋能技术，允许以各种形式高效、大规模地分发益虫。”

**温室创新：快速安全的遮阳**
在园艺领域，一个迅速扩展的应用是使用无人机在温室屋顶上施用遮阳涂层。这种做法可以防止夏季过热，比传统方法更安全、更快捷。Marras确认了这一服务的巨大潜力：“通过一天内覆盖6.7公顷，考虑到支付给承包商的每公顷费用，并且无论如何，无人机服务比传统方法更便宜、风险更小，因此所有相关方——无论是农民还是操作员——都能受益。”

**安全、飞行终止系统及即将到来的法规变化**
操作这种尺寸的无人机需要严格的ENAC授权。Aermatica3D通过完全在意大利制造Agras系列的飞行终止系统（FTS）克服了这一障碍。最后，关于植物保护处理全面监管绿灯的期待已久的时间表，Marras向行业保证。限制的解除已在法律中规定，卫生部在Aermatica3D支持的试验期间收集的数据的支持下，“将在未来几个月内发布法令，为下一季的田间作业铺平道路。”
featuredReason: 本文精准聚焦无人机在智慧畜牧与精准农业中的实际应用，涵盖施肥、生物防治、温室遮阳等场景，技术细节丰富（如多光谱测绘、FTS系统），并包含企业案例与法规进展，对行业从业者具有较高的参考价值和启发性。

 OPEN
 

 
 

 

 
 
 

 
 
 
 

 
 Tuesday, 21 July 2026
 

 
 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
 Aermatica3D

 The drone revolution in precision farming

 

 
 On 21 July 2026, Cascina Scarampa in Zeme (PV) will host the "Demo-Farm-Next-Vision" demonstration event, which will focus on the enormous potential of aerial fertiliser application. Among the key participants is Aermatica3D, an Italian pioneer in the sector, which will showcase the cutting-edge applications of drones in agriculture. Whilst agricultural drones have historically been associated with large cereal plots or rice fields, Aermatica3D's solutions now offer extraordinary and targeted opportunities for the fruit and vegetable sector too, ranging from viticulture and orchards to specialised and intensive crops such as industrial tomatoes.

 © Aermatica3D Complementarity and efficiency in open fields and between rowsAermatica3D, a DJI Enterprise system integrator and DJI Agriculture maintenance centre, offers fleets of drones capable of revolutionising crop treatments such as the DJI Agras T100 and the DJI Agras T70P. These machines enable the application of biostimulants and liquid fertilisers without causing any soil compaction or trampling. As Paolo Marras (pictured), General Manager of Aermatica3D, explains regarding the adoption of these technologies in intensive farming: "Drones are tools that complement traditional methods, becoming essential in all those situations where traditional equipment cannot operate."

 Thanks to technological developments and regulatory changes, their use is growing not only in complex hillside vineyards (where agile models such as the DJI Agras T25P can navigate slopes of up to 50°), but also in open fields. The key to success, Marras continues, "is the ability to operate with low application rates per hectare, thanks to the efficiency of the distribution system, which utilises the airflow generated by the propellers to sustain the drone's flight."

 Multispectral mapping: A measurable ROIOperational efficiency goes hand in hand with sustainability. Multispectral mapping allows for the real-time assessment of plant vigour and water stress, translating the data into prescription maps for variable-rate applications. This transition towards Agriculture 4.0 leads to a tangible return on investment (ROI). "With the aim of applying the right amount of product to the areas where it is needed, this approach saves time and product, optimising treatments," emphasises Marras. Satisfaction is such that farms are rapidly scaling up their use of drones, whilst contractors are expanding their fleets from season to season to cover ever-increasing areas.

 © Aermatica3D

 Biological control: Drones as an enabling technologyIn addition to liquids, Aermatica3D has developed proprietary kits to directly support biological control, such as the D-BOX for insect eggs, the P/G-KIT for assisted pollination and the K-KIT for the release of live beneficial insects. In this regard, Aermatica3D collaborates regularly with leading biotech companies. "A significant example of this is our collaboration with Koppert on the development of a drone-optimised kit for the distribution of adult beneficial insects," says Marras, adding that "Drones are an enabling technology for the widespread adoption of biocontrol techniques, allowing for the efficient, large-scale distribution of beneficial insects in their various forms."

 Innovation in greenhouses: Dast and safe shadingIn horticulture, one rapidly expanding application is the use of drones to apply shading coatings to greenhouse roofs. This practice prevents excessive heat build-up in summer in a far safer and quicker way than with traditional methods. Marras confirms the great potential of this service: "By managing to cover 6. 7 hectares in a single day, given the per-hectare fee paid to the contractor and bearing in mind that, in any case, the drone service is cheaper and less risky than the traditional method, it follows that all parties involved - both farmers and operators - stand to gain."

 © Aermatica3D

 Safety, FTS and forthcoming regulatory changesOperating drones of this size requires strict ENAC authorisations. Aermatica3D has overcome this hurdle by manufacturing the Flight Termination System (FTS) for the Agras range entirely in Italy. Finally, regarding the long-awaited timeline for a full regulatory green light for plant protection treatments, Marras reassures the sector. The lifting of restrictions is already provided for by law and the Ministry of Health, bolstered by the data collected during trials supported by Aermatica3D, "will publish the decree in the coming months, paving the way for field operations to begin for the next season."

 For more information:Aermatica3DVia Tettamanti 1Colverde (CO) 22041 – Italy+39 031 552070 [email protected]www.aermatica.com
